title: Bee v2.7.0 auto-races legacy and wrapped feed versions so adapters no longer pick versions manually
content:
## Claim

Starting in Bee v2.7.0, feed reads **auto-race legacy and wrapped feed formats** and return whichever resolves first — adapters no longer need to choose a feed version explicitly.

## Evidence

- Both legacy and wrapped feed versions now race automatically on every read
- The response header `Swarm-Feed-Resolved-Version` reports which format won
- The `swarm-feed-legacy-resolve` query parameter was **removed** — code that still passes it will break
- The adapter never used that query param, so we are unaffected

## Related v2.7.x adapter-relevant changes

- **AutoTLS / WSS**: secure WebSocket via `p2p-wss-enable` — relevant when we wire up PSS/GSOC subscriptions
- **Redundancy validation**: `/bytes` and `/bzz` now reject invalid `Swarm-Redundancy-Level` values — we don't set it, so fine
- **`POST /tags`** no longer requires a body — simplifies upload progress tracking
- **`/reservestate`** now includes `reserveCapacityDoubling`
- **v2.7.1 TopUpBatch nil-pointer fix** — prevents crashes when topping up stamps on light nodes (critical for StampManager)

Our production node runs **Bee v2.7.1**.

## Why it matters

Codifies a forward-compatibility win (one fewer branch in read paths) and catalogs the v2.7.x changes the adapter inherits. Also a reminder to run `/health` version checks before debugging feed-read mysteries — bee-js targets Bee 2.5, and some 2.7-only behaviors (like auto-racing) are implicit.
description: Both feed versions race; the Swarm-Feed-Resolved-Version response header reveals the winner. The swarm-feed-legacy-resolve query param was removed.
